models.name: Kimi K3 2.8T-A50B (MoE)
models.arch: MoE (896E/16A, KDA hybrid + MLA eff., est. cfg)

models.name: Jais-2-70B (Arabic)
models.arch: Dense (GQA, est. cfg) · from scratch, Cerebras-trained

models.name: Jais-2-8B (Arabic)
models.arch: Dense (GQA, est. cfg) · from scratch, Cerebras-trained

models.name: Cohere Command A+ 218B-A25B (MoE)
models.arch: MoE (25B active, est. cfg) · Apache 2.0, vision-in

quants.name: INT4
quants.use: GPTQ/AWQ deployment (+~6% group scales not counted)

cases.name: Simple chatbot
cases.note: Short support chat. 20 tok/s outruns reading speed; a 250-tok reply streams in ~13 s.

cases.name: Simple RAG
cases.note: Single-pass RAG. TTFT budget includes retrieval + rerank (~400 ms) ahead of prefill.

cases.name: Advanced RAG
cases.note: Multi-hop agentic RAG: 16-32K context, ~800 thinking/tool-plan tokens per call on reasoning models, ~6 LLM calls per answer.

cases.name: Internal GPT / Copilot
cases.note: Enterprise assistant with doc retrieval, multi-turn; prefix caching assumed for history.

cases.name: Document Q&A
cases.note: Long doc in context with a light grounded-verification budget (~500 thinking tokens); TTFT is dominated by the 32K prefill.

cases.name: Document generation
cases.note: Long-form drafting. Users skim, not read along: 40 tok/s, a 2K draft streams in ~50 s.

cases.name: Code generation
cases.note: IDE completion / small edits: 10-100 token suggestions; anything slower than ~2 s gets discarded, so P95 is tight.

cases.name: Code agent (Cline/Cursor-style)
cases.note: Repo-aware coding agent: 32-64K append-only context, ~2K tokens per tool step (thinking + diff), ~30 steps per 20-30 min task.

traffic.pct: ~100%: an engaged user keeps one agent stream busy

cases.name: Voice agent (real-time)
cases.note: Telephony/IVR. Voice-to-voice budget needs LLM TTFT under ~300 ms; 50 tok/s feeds sentence-chunked TTS without gaps.

cases.name: Reasoning agent (light)
cases.note: ~2K hidden CoT + short answer. Nobody reads CoT: decode speed is the latency lever, so target 60 tok/s.

cases.name: Reasoning agent (heavy)
cases.note: ~8K CoT budget (math/coding/research). 8.5K tokens at 60 tok/s is a ~142 s physical floor.

cases.name: Video summarization
cases.note: Chunked VLM captioning then aggregate; ~30-47K vision tokens per minute of video at native sampling.

cases.name: Long-doc analysis (legal/financial)
cases.note: 64-128K contracts/filings. TTFT is the long prefill (~5 s at 64K tuned); prefix caching helps repeat queries.

cases.name: Offline batch / extract
cases.note: Batch jobs (extraction, classification): 24 h job SLA, no interactive SLOs; size for throughput at max batch.

traffic.pct: queue-set: enter jobs in flight, not people

cases.name: Deep research agent (async)
cases.note: Multi-step autonomous research: ~2K thinking tokens per call across ~40 calls (~35 min per task, ~100K output tokens total).

cases.name: Medical imaging report assistant
cases.note: VLM reads a study (1-3K image tokens in context) and drafts findings/impression. Clinician-interactive.

cases.name: Clinical knowledge assistant
cases.note: Grounded medical Q&A with a real verification budget (~1K thinking tokens); guardrails on both directions.

cases.name: Real-time video analytics
cases.note: VLM monitors streams in ~10 s clips (~8K vision tokens each); alert within seconds. Concurrency = camera streams.

cases.name: Translation / localization
cases.note: Segment/page translation, roughly symmetric input and output. Subtitle mode needs segments under ~2 s.

cases.name: Contact-center agent assist
cases.note: Live suggestions while a human talks: transcript + KB in context, tiny outputs, utterance-to-suggestion under ~2.5 s.

traffic.pct: 100%: live assisted calls are the concurrency

kinds.label: Embeddings
kinds.role: turns text into vectors for retrieval

models.name: nomic-embed-text v1.5
models.note: Smallest footprint; CPU-viable

models.name: granite-embedding-english-r2
models.note: ModernBERT encoder, 8k context; ~144 long docs/s on one H100, Apache 2.0

models.name: EmbeddingGemma 300M
models.note: Best MTEB Multilingual under 500M; Matryoshka 768-128 dims, edge-viable QAT builds

models.name: BGE-M3
models.note: Multilingual dense+sparse retrieval; ~60k tokens/s per instance

models.name: snowflake-arctic-embed-l-v2.0
models.note: 8k context with MRL 256-dim truncation; leads sub-1B open models on BEIR/MIRACL

models.name: Qwen3-Embedding 0.6B
models.note: Lightweight MTEB top-tier

models.name: Qwen3-Embedding 4B
models.note: Near-8B MTEB scores at half the VRAM; 32k context, 2560 dims

models.name: Qwen3-Embedding 8B
models.note: MTEB multilingual leader; needs a large slice

models.name: gte-multilingual-reranker-base
models.note: Encoder-only 8k cross-encoder, ~10x faster than LLM rerankers; 70+ languages

models.name: bge-reranker-v2-m3
models.note: Cross-encoder; ~150-250 ms per top-50 rerank

models.name: Qwen3-Reranker 0.6B
models.note: Lightweight cross-encoder

models.name: mxbai-rerank-large-v2
models.note: RL-tuned Qwen cross-encoder; BEIR 57.5 tops open rerankers, 100+ languages

models.name: zerank-1-small
models.note: Pairwise ELO-trained; beats cohere-rerank-v3.5 on finance/legal/code, Apache 2.0

models.name: Qwen3-Reranker 4B
models.note: Nearly matches the 8B on half the slice; 32k context

models.name: Qwen3-Reranker 8B
models.note: ~31 req/s under vLLM; needs a large slice

models.name: Moonshine Base
models.note: 61M MIT edge ASR, RTFx ~566; realtime even on CPU

models.name: Parakeet-TDT 0.6B v3
models.note: Batch-throughput champion (RTFx ~3,300)

models.name: Whisper large-v3-turbo
models.note: ~130x real-time; the serving default

models.name: Kyutai STT 1B en_fr
models.note: Streaming-native (0.5 s delay) with semantic VAD; 400 concurrent streams per H100

models.name: Canary-1B v2
models.note: Adds speech translation

models.name: Canary-Qwen-2.5B
models.note: Open ASR leaderboard leader (5.63% WER en), RTFx ~418; LLM mode does post-ASR QA

models.name: Voxtral Mini 3B
models.note: Apache 2.0 transcription + translation + audio QA in 8 languages; 30-min files

models.name: Granite Speech 3.3 8B
models.note: Apache 2.0 multilingual ASR+AST at 5.74% leaderboard WER

models.name: Kitten TTS Nano
models.note: 15M params, sub-25MB, Apache 2.0; realtime on CPU, 8 voices

models.name: Kokoro-82M
models.note: ~15x real-time; Apache license

models.name: Fun-CosyVoice3-0.5B
models.note: Zero-shot multilingual cloning, bidirectional streaming, ~150 ms first packet

models.name: Chatterbox TTS
models.note: MIT zero-shot cloning with emotion control; preferred over ElevenLabs in blind evals

models.name: Dia-1.6B
models.note: Two-speaker dialogue with nonverbals in one pass; Apache 2.0

models.name: Orpheus-3B
models.note: Streaming, expressive; Apache license

models.name: PaddleOCR PP-OCRv5
models.note: ~190 pages/min per instance; plain text

models.name: Granite-Docling 258M
models.note: One tiny VLM replaces the layout+table+formula pipeline; DocTags output, CPU-viable

models.name: PaddleOCR-VL
models.note: OmniDocBench leader across 109 languages; NaViT + ERNIE decoder, serves on vLLM

models.name: MonkeyOCR-pro-1.2B
models.note: Structure-Recognition-Relation parser; 1.4 pages/s on H800

models.name: DeepSeek-OCR
models.note: Optical context compression cuts vision tokens ~10x; ~200k pages/day per A100, MIT

models.name: dots.ocr 3B
models.note: Layout-aware VLM parser; MIT license

models.name: olmOCR-2 7B
models.note: Highest-fidelity document parsing

models.name: Qwen3Guard-Gen-0.6B
models.note: Cheapest guard tier; 119-language moderation with severity levels, Apache 2.0

models.name: Llama Guard 3 1B
models.note: Input/output moderation; CPU-viable

models.name: ShieldGemma 2 4B
models.note: IMAGE-only safety classifier for VLM inputs / image-gen outputs; does not moderate text

models.name: Qwen3Guard-Gen-4B
models.note: Mid-tier 119-language moderator, SOTA multilingual safety; adds severity tiers

models.name: Llama Guard 3 8B FP8
models.note: Higher accuracy; fits a 10 GB slice

models.name: Granite Guardian 3.3 8B
models.note: Moderation + RAG groundedness + function-call hallucination checks; Apache 2.0

models.name: Llama Guard 4 12B
models.note: Natively multimodal text+image on the MLCommons 14-hazard taxonomy; single-GPU

resilience.label: A/A ½+½
resilience.long: Active/Active split · N across two sites (1x)

resilience.label: A/A ½+½ +1
resilience.long: Active/Active split · N across two sites + one spare (N+1)

resilience.label: A/A ½+½ +1/site
resilience.long: Active/Active split · N across two sites + spare per site (N+2)

resilience.label: A/A N+1
resilience.long: Active/Active · N+1 in each of two sites (2N+2)
